title: pseudogynoxys sprucei
intro: Pseudogynoxys sprucei, also known as Andean tree daisy, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, and is also known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pseudogynoxys sprucei include its ability to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, dysentery, and other gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be consumed fresh or dried to extract its medicinal compounds.
